title: Person wearing KKK robes in R.I. was pulling a social media stunt, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News A West Warwick resident captured video of the white-robed figure wandering around town early Monday morning.
Police in Rhode Island say they’ve confirmed that videos of a person walking around a town in a Ku Klux Klan robe and hood were part of a hoax for social media, not the work of an organized group.
Social media videos appeared to show the person walking around West Warwick while dressed in white robes and a pointed hood.

title: Report: Nearly half of immigrants arrested by ICE in Mass. had no criminal conviction or charge
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News Data shows that 45 percent of immigration arrests since 2025 involved people without criminal convictions or pending criminal cases.
FILE - Federal law enforcement officers with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO) conduct a traffic stop and detain people, Nov.
17, 2025, in Washington.

title: Boston FBI division recovers 17th century urn stolen from Italian church years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Arts The urn was reportedly stolen from the Church of San Michele Arcangelo di Cangiano sometime between 2012 and 2022.
The urn was recovered from a Northeast antiques dealer who bought it from an Italian dealer, the Boston office of the FBI said.
Boston FBI The Boston division of the FBI recovered a 17th-century reliquary urn that was stolen years ago from an Italian church, officials said.

title: Conn. woman allegedly sexually assaulted dog, made pornographic videos, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News The woman allegedly sent another person three videos depicting her making sexual contact with her dog, according to authorities.
By Darin Zullo April 30, 2026 | 7:37 PM 2 minutes to read A Connecticut woman has been charged with sexually assaulting her dog and creating pornography of the alleged assault, according to police.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, of Meriden, Conn.

title: Roofer dead in fall from home in Southborough
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News "Our deepest condolences go out to the friends and family of the individual who lost his life following yesterday’s incident,” the Southborough Police Department and Fire Department said.
By Carson Lyle April 30, 2026 | 6:28 PM 1 minute to read A roofer died Wednesday afternoon after he fell from atop a Southborough home, local public safety officials said.

“Our deepest condolences go out to the friends and family of the individual who lost his life following yesterday’s incident,” the Southborough Police Department and Fire Department said in a joint press release on Facebook.
